economic_notes: Band participates in Mi'kmaq Wind4all Communities Limited Partnership and Beaubassin Mi'kmaq Wind Management Ltd Solar Photovoltaic System. Also holds portfolio investments at fair value of $23,810,186. Holds 6.14% promissory note receivable from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership of $34,668,442 maturing 2051. Commercial revenue from Clearwater Seafoods Inc. relationship. Member of E'sukitimkewey Limited Partnership and Mi'kmaq Resource Development Partnership with 12 other NS First Nations.
notes: Fiscal year ends March 31, 2023 (not 2024). Consolidated entities include Pictou Landing Band Council, Pictou Landing Housing Fund, A'Se'K Seven Generations Trust, and Victoria Corner Market. Demand loans of $7,114,102 treated separately from long-term debt of $38,082,974; annual_debt_service reflects repayments of both demand debt ($1,485,919) and long-term debt ($1,114,524) totalling $2,600,443. FNFA Revenue Bonds of ~$35.7M are the dominant long-term debt instrument. Tangible capital assets net book value not explicitly stated in extracted text. No schedule of remuneration included in extracted text. Government transfers appear to be entirely from ISC based on note 18 reference, though DFO receivables suggest some DFO funding may be included in government transfers. Large promissory note receivable of $34.7M from First Nations Fisheries Interest Group Partnership is a significant financial asset not visible in standard balance sheet ratios. Adopted PS 3280 Asset Retirement Obligations and PS 3450 Financial Instruments effective April 1, 2022.
